items.title: EU to ramp up Meta probe into addictive design: Bloomberg
items.summary: STORY: The European Commission plans to ramp up its probe into Meta, a Bloomberg report said. The EU alleges the social media giant designs its platforms to be addictive to children. The report said the bloc is preparing preliminary findings but has not set a date for the announcement.It’s not the first time Meta has faced scrutiny from the commission. It first opened a probe into the company under the Digital Services Act in May 2024, due to concerns it had not adequately addressed risks to children.In April it charged Meta with breaching its tech rules…And said the Instagram owner must do more to block children under 13 from accessing social networks. The report said the EU is considering curbs similar to those announced by the UK and other countries after an expert panel delivers recommendations next month.Meta and the European Commission did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment. Reuters could not independently verify the report.Meanwhile, a source told Reuters the social media giant is pushing for legal protection in Washington. The source said Meta is lobbying U.S. lawmakers for language that would shield social media firms from certain claims tied to minors’ safety and privacy.

items.title: Palantir (PLTR) Teams Up With Zeta Global To Build AI Marketing Infrastructure
items.summary: Palantir Technologies (NasdaqGS:PLTR) and Zeta Global announced a new partnership to build unified AI infrastructure for marketing and operational intelligence. The collaboration focuses on rearchitecting Zeta’s Data Cloud on Palantir’s Foundry platform, integrating data governance, real-time AI decisioning, and marketing execution.
